subject: Civics
chapter_name: Parliament and the Making of Laws
overall_learning_objectives: Analyse the role of Parliament in a democratic system
Understand the structure and functions of the two houses of Parliament
Evaluate the responsibilities of Members of Parliament
Examine the role of opposition in ensuring accountability
Understand the law-making process in detail
Evaluate how public issues influence laws in a democracy

lesson_plan.title: Role of Parliament in Democracy
student_activities: Analyse how representatives speak for people in government
Discuss why citizens need representatives in a large country

lesson_plan.title: Structure of the Indian Parliament
student_activities: Draw a diagram showing the two houses of Parliament
Compare the functions of Lok Sabha and Rajya Sabha

lesson_plan.title: Members of Parliament and Representation
student_activities: Discuss how MPs raise issues of their constituencies
Write a short explanation of how MPs represent citizens

lesson_plan.title: Role of Opposition and Accountability
student_activities: Participate in a short debate on a public issue
Explain why questioning government decisions is important

lesson_plan.title: Process of Law Making
topics_covered: Steps involved in passing a law
student_activities: Create a flowchart showing how a bill becomes a law
Discuss why discussions are important before passing laws

lesson_plan.title: Public Issues and Law Making – Revision
topics_covered: Connection between public issues and laws
student_activities: Analyse a public issue and suggest how a law could address it
Participate in a quiz covering the chapter

teacher_tip: Encourage students to analyse current public issues and think about how laws and parliamentary debates can address those issues in a democratic system.
